items.title: US economy undershoots forecasts with 57,000 jobs added in June
items.description: Hiring slows after three-month streak of overperformance

items.title: Tesla deliveries climb 25% as fuel price surge boosts Europe demand
items.description: Strong second-quarter sales in Europe and China help offset weak US figures hit by end of EV tax credits

items.title: Blue Owl hit by $4.7bn of redemption requests as investor exodus persists
items.description: Withdrawal requests at 20 private credit funds tracked by FT totalled more than $22bn in second quarter

items.title: Merz unveils €10bn tax cuts to revive German growth
items.description: Ruling coalition overcomes divisions to agree longer Sunday opening hours and tighter sick-leave rules

items.title: Fear, loathing and ice cream pricing
items.description: Japan’s food industry is a shopfront for the vulnerabilities of a weak currency

items.title: Russia unleashes huge bombardment of Kyiv
items.description: Attack of 74 missiles and nearly 500 drones follows Zelenskyy’s warning that Moscow is preparing ‘massive strike’

items.title: OpenAI proposes handing Trump administration 5% stake
items.description: Sam Altman’s start-up in early talks for a public ownership deal as political pressure rises

items.title: The economics of women’s weight
items.description: A new study suggests weight-loss drugs help women find work — and prompt men to leave their partners

items.title: Trump’s $1bn plan to remake America’s capital
items.description: The US president calls it a ‘gift’ to the nation. Critics say he is racing past checks to stamp his mark on the capital

items.title: This is how we can make AI safe for everyone
items.description: The labs develop the technology, but citizens and their elected representatives must make the rules
